ddls:
CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the dataset was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the dataset was last updated */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the dataset (e.g., internal, external, user-uploaded) */,
  "size" TEXT /* Size of the dataset in bytes */,
  "format" TEXT /* Format of the dataset (e.g., CSV, JSON, Parquet) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being used */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being used */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., MAE, RMSE) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "improvement" REAL /* Improvement in performance compared to baseline */,
  "dataset_version" TEXT /* Version of the dataset used for the performance metric */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the performance metric was recorded */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user recording the metric */,
  "custom_metric" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the metric is user-defined */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the model was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the model was last updated */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the model */,
  "hyperparameters" TEXT /* JSON column to store model hyperparameters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, researcher, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user account was last updated */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user last logged in */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, inactive, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being accessed */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the dataset or model */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the dataset or model was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the access event was recorded */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the dataset or model */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client accessing the dataset or model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)
